nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal waters; seagrass beds, estuaries, lagoons, harbors, mangroves, and seaward reefs. Depth range 0–68 m, typically 0–8 m. Marine and brackish tolerant.

diet.notes: Requires live or frozen mysid shrimp, enriched brine shrimp (artemia), amphipods, and small crustaceans. Must be fed multiple times daily (4–5 feedings). Fry require live copepods (e.g. Tisbe biminiensis); adults accept frozen mysis after acclimation. Slow feeders unable to compete with fast-moving tankmates.

imageCandidates.notes: Photographer: Emőke Dénes. Photographed at Sea Life Centre Hannover, Germany, December 2019. File dimensions: 2595 × 3459 px. Yellow morph individual clearly identified as Hippocampus kuda.
Photographer: J. Greenfield; location: Indonesia. FishBase species gallery image. License not explicitly stated on page.
Product photo from LiveAquaria Estuary Seahorse Tank Bred listing. Clean, high-resolution retailer image. Identified as H. cf kuda (Sri Lanka captive-bred stock).

summary: Peaceful, slow-moving seahorse from Indo-Pacific estuaries and seagrass beds. Requires dedicated species tank with calm water, hitching posts, and multiple daily feedings of live or frozen mysid shrimp. Advanced care.
careNotes: Hippocampus kuda (Estuary Seahorse / Spotted Seahorse) requires a species-specific or pipefish-only aquarium — competition from faster-moving fish will cause starvation. Minimum tank 50 gallons (189 L) for a pair; add 10–15 gallons per additional pair. Water flow should be low to moderate; strong flow stresses seahorses. Provide ample hitching posts: branching gorgonians, artificial coral, algae, or macroalgae. Feed 4–5 times daily: frozen mysis shrimp and enriched artemia are the staple diet; live copepods and amphipods support natural foraging. Maintain stable water quality — temperature 22–27°C (72–78°F); avoid temperatures above 80°F (27°C) as this increases susceptibility to Vibrio infections. pH 8.1–8.4, specific gravity 1.021–1.025 (26–33 ppt), dKH 8–12. IUCN status: Vulnerable (VU); CITES Appendix II — prefer tank-bred specimens. Source disagreement on minimum tank: LiveAquaria Kuda page lists 30 gal, Estuary Seahorse page lists 50 gal; conservative 50 gal used. Source disagreement on temperature ceiling: FishBase environmental range extends to 29°C; aquarium sources recommend 25–26°C max; conservative 27°C used here. Species may be encountered labeled as H. cf kuda (captive-bred Sri Lanka stock) — taxonomic status under review.
breedingNotes: Ovoviviparous; male carries eggs in a ventral brood pouch. Gestation 20–28 days; brood size 20–1,000 fry depending on male size and conditions. Monogamy not confirmed in laboratory settings. Breeding in captivity is achievable but demanding: fry require live zooplankton (rotifers, copepods such as Tisbe biminiensis) and frequent feeding. Classified as 'difficult' breeding difficulty.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Estuary Seahorse Tank Bred (Sri Lanka) product page. Primary source for care parameters, tank requirements, and diet. Note: parent URL in manifest was the seahorses collection page; actual species page used.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (max 30 cm TL confirmed), temperature range (21.3–29°C), native range, taxonomy (Syngnathidae / Syngnathiformes), and diet (zooplankton/carnivore). Minor disagreement on temperature ceiling: FishBase max 29°C vs LiveAquaria recommended max ~25.6°C (78°F); conservative 27°C used.

additional.name: LiveAquaria (Kuda Seahorse, Captive-Bred)
additional.notes: Second LiveAquaria listing for same species (H. kuda captive-bred). Confirmed: care level Difficult, peaceful temperament, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, reef compatible. This listing gives 30-gal minimum vs 50-gal on the Estuary page; conservative 50-gal used.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Hippocampus kuda)
additional.notes: Confirmed size range (17–30 cm), habitat description (estuaries, lagoons, seagrass beds), temperature (72–77°F / 22–25°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity tolerance (18–36 ppt). Used for range and habitat cross-verification.

fish.breedingNotes: Male broods eggs in ventral pouch; gestation 20–28 days; brood size 20–1,000 fry. Fry require live zooplankton. Captive breeding is achievable in dedicated species tanks.
